title: Open-Source AI Agents
summary: Reviewed published projections with public source links and AI agent or developer workflow relevance.
inclusionCriteria: Projects must come from reviewed published projections, include public source links, and match a finite collection definition.
whyIncluded: Aurora Code and Nucleus ML are published projections with public source links and AI workflow context.
methodologyNote: Collections are finite reviewed groups. They are not generated from arbitrary filters, query parameters, external crawls, or bulk templates.

title: RAG Projects
summary: Reviewed published projections connected to retrieval, vector search, or AI infrastructure workflows.
inclusionCriteria: Projects must be reviewed published projections with public source links and retrieval or search infrastructure relevance.
whyIncluded: Nucleus ML and VectorBase are published projections with public documentation and infrastructure context.
methodologyNote: This collection is a finite discovery surface. It does not recalculate scores or infer private adoption data.

title: AI Outbound
summary: Reviewed published projections with public product context for outreach, analytics, or operations workflows.
inclusionCriteria: Projects must be reviewed published projections with public product context and public source links.
whyIncluded: Pulse Analytics and ComplyKit are published projections with public product and operations context.
methodologyNote: Collection membership is reviewed and finite. It does not verify customers, revenue, financing, or private outcomes.

title: AI Tool Alternatives
summary: Reviewed published projections that have finite discovery links to related project alternatives.
inclusionCriteria: Projects must be reviewed published projections and must appear only in this finite collection definition.
whyIncluded: Aurora Code, VectorBase, and ComplyKit are published projections with public source links and related discovery context.
methodologyNote: Alternatives context is neutral discovery support. It does not order, endorse, or predict outcomes for projects.
